data.narrative:
Xtend is a Series B defense and security company founded by Aviv Shapira that has raised $70M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparables: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, with particular weight given to 5 same-stage, same-sector private peers including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $133M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 closest comparables in Defense & Security at Series B stage yields a median valuation of $1.2B, with observed peer valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B.

The two methods produce materially divergent signals. The triangulated analysis supports a range of $113M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $666M. The wide dispersion reflects methodological tension: stage-progression anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while peer-median reflects elevated valuations in the frontier defense sector. Key uncertainties include whether peer comparables represent truly equivalent risk and growth profiles, and whether recent defense spending tailwinds have inflated peer valuations above equilibrium levels. Additional data on revenue, growth rate, and customer concentration would narrow the range materially.
